food_name: Jack 'n Jill Piattos Cheese Flavoured Potato Crisps (1 pack, 60g)
aliases: 芝士薯片 (Zhī Shì Shǔ Piàn)

chinese: 芝士薯片 (Zhī Shì Shǔ Piàn)

region_of_origin: Philippines (manufactured by Universal Robina Corporation / Jack 'n Jill); widely distributed in Singapore
food_subgroup: Extruded potato-flour crisp snack with cheese flavouring; ultra-processed packaged snack

ontology.foodon_label: potato chip (closest parent: extruded snack food)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific class for branded extruded potato-flour crisps. FOODON_03315595 (potato chip) is used as the closest defensible parent. Piattos are extruded from reconstituted potato flour, not sliced from fresh potato.
ontology.foodex2_description: Potato crisps / chips (extruded snack, closest FoodEx2 parent for extruded potato-flour snack products)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers potato crisps broadly; no specific code exists for extruded reconstituted-potato-flour disc-shaped crisps. Used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for branded extruded potato snack products. SNOMED CT is not used for this record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard single-serve pack of Piattos Cheese Flavoured Potato Crisps weighs 60g and is the typical full-pack consumption unit sold in Singapore supermarkets, convenience stores, and school canteens.

nutrient_source.title: Jack 'n Jill Piattos Cheese Flavoured Potato Crisps — Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP)
nutrient_source.organization: Universal Robina Corporation / Jack 'n Jill — Singapore import product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-market product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as declared by the manufacturer Universal Robina Corporation. Per-100g values derived from the NIP per-serving (60g) declaration. This constitutes a USDA Branded Food equivalent for a packaged product with a verified label. Values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entry for extruded potato snacks (FDC ID 1100535, potato chips, flavoured) for plausibility; all values are within expected range.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Extruded Potato-Based Snack Crisps — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous food: extruded potato crisps, GI ~70–75
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jack 'n Jill Piattos Cheese specifically. GI of 72 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database range for extruded potato-based crisps (GI ~70–75, average ~72). The extrusion-dehydration process gelatinises potato starch more completely than slicing, producing a higher GI than traditional potato chips. GL per 60g serving = (72 × 65g carbohydrate per 100g × 60g serving / 100) / 100 = 28.1. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content: 767mg per 100g; 460mg per 60g pack (23% of 2,000mg/day recommended limit)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Piattos consumption; one 60g pack delivers 460mg sodium, representing nearly a quarter of the daily recommended sodium limit, and the sodium-to-potassium ratio remains unfavourable despite the potato-flour potassium content (~336mg per 60g pack).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) and high glycemic load (~28 per 60g pack) combined with high saturated fat (7.5g per pack)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Piattos are not suitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the high GI (~72) and high GL (~28 per pack) from fully gelatinised extruded potato starch cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation, and the concurrent high saturated fat load (12.5g/100g) further impairs insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (12.5g/100g; 7.5g per 60g pack) from palm oil, high sodium (767mg/100g), and ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid Piattos; the combination of high saturated fat from palm oil (raising LDL cholesterol), high sodium (elevating blood pressure), and the ultra-processed nature of the product makes it a high-risk snack for cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat, high sodium, ultra-processed with no anti-inflammatory nutritional value
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Piattos are not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; the high palm-oil saturated fat content and ultra-processed formulation provide no anti-inflammatory benefit and may exacerbate systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jack 'n Jill Piattos Cheese Flavoured Potato Crisps are a Filipino-origin ultra-processed extruded snack manufactured by Universal Robina Corporation (Jack 'n Jill brand) and widely sold in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, convenience stores, and school canteens. Unlike traditional potato chips sliced from fresh potatoes, Piattos are made from reconstituted dehydrated potato flour that is extruded into a distinctive ring or disc shape, then fried in palm oil and coated with an intense cheese flavour powder. The result is a uniformly crunchy, highly palatable snack with a strong savoury-cheesy aroma. A standard single-serve pack weighs 60g. Nutritionally, Piattos are calorically dense at 530 kcal per 100g (318 kcal per 60g pack), with a high fat content of 28.3g/100g — predominantly saturated fat (12.5g/100g) from palm oil — and a high carbohydrate load of 65g/100g. The extrusion-dehydration process fully gelatinises the potato starch, producing a high glycemic index of approximately 72 and a glycemic load of ~28 per pack, making Piattos glycemically harmful for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is high at 767mg/100g (460mg per 60g pack), representing 23% of the 2,000mg/day recommended daily limit, making this snack unsuitable for hypertension management. The saturated fat content raises LDL cholesterol risk, making Piattos a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease. Piattos are labelled halal and vegetarian; the cheese flavouring contains milk derivatives. The product is widely considered gluten-free by formulation. Piattos are a culturally embedded snack in the Singapore school canteen and convenience store context, often consumed as a full-pack single sitting.
common_user_queries: Jack n Jill Piattos fat content

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-market product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) as declared by the manufacturer Universal Robina Corporation / Jack 'n Jill. Per-100g values are derived from the verified per-serving (60g) NIP declaration. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for extruded potato-based crisps (GI ~70–75) with confidence 0.9. All values are plausible and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central extruded potato snack entries.
notes: 1. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per Singapore product NIP declaration by Universal Robina Corporation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per NIP; product contains no animal-derived fat (palm oil only). vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0: confirmed zero or below detection threshold per NIP; extruded potato snack with cheese powder coating contains negligible amounts of these vitamins. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Singapore product NIP and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not declared on the Singapore product NIP and is not available from the approved sources consulted. 3. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for extruded potato-based crisps (range 70–75); no direct GI measurement for Jack 'n Jill Piattos Cheese specifically has been published. GL per 60g serving calculated as (72 × 65 × 0.60) / 100 = 28.1. 4. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are used as closest defensible parents; no exact ontology codes exist for branded extruded reconstituted-potato-flour disc-shaped crisps. 5. This product is classified as Singapore-mainstream (widely sold in Singapore) but is Filipino in origin; multilingual naming follows the Singaporean dishes rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given its mainstream Singapore market presence.
